description: In a kitchen, Chandler stands facing Eddie, with both hands resting on Eddie's shoulders. Eddie says, "What I'm doing here."
Eddie looks back at Chandler as audience laughter is audible.
Chandler drops his hands from Eddie's shoulders. "Now, that can't be smart," he says.
Eddie glances away and then looks back. Audience laughter builds as Chandler gestures with his right hand.

"So we're just going to take this guy right off you and put him here in Mr." Chandler says, reaching toward Eddie's chest and miming pulling something away, then bringing his hand toward his own waist.
"...Pocket?" Chandler finishes, tucking his hand into his pants pocket.
Chandler turns away, smiling, and walks toward the counter. Eddie watches him. Audience laughter plays.
Chandler picks up an orange from a bowl and tosses it in the air. "Tangelo," he says, turning back toward Eddie.
Chandler tosses the orange to Eddie. The orange bounces off Eddie's chest and falls to the floor with a thud. Audience laughter is audible.

Chandler gestures with his hands open. The setting changes to the exterior of an apartment building at night; sirens wail faintly. The setting changes again to a bedroom, where Richard stands facing Monica, who sits on the edge of a bed.
"That's it? That's the giant number you were afraid to tell me?" Richard asks, extending his hands outward. "Well, yeah," Monica says, resting her hands in her lap.
"Well, that's not bad at all. You had me thinking it was, like a fleet," Richard says, placing his hands near his waist and leaning forward slightly.
Monica smiles, and Richard sits down on the edge of the bed next to her.
"Are you really okay with it?" Monica asks, smiling. "Oh, honey, I'm fine," Richard replies. Audience laughter plays.
"Yay," Monica says, grinning.
Richard exhales audibly and falls backward onto the mattress, his arms resting by his sides. Monica remains seated, looking down at him.
"Okay, about that two?" Monica says, turning her head toward him.
Richard lifts his head slightly from the bed.
"What?" Richard asks. Audience laughter is audible.
Richard sits up, swinging his arms forward to lift himself.
"All right," Richard says.
He turns to face Monica.
"what about my two?" Richard asks. "Well, it just seems like a really small number," Monica replies, smiling slightly.
"Right?" Richard says.
Richard gestures with both hands, palms up.
"Well, don't you have a lot of wild oats to sow?" Monica asks, raising her eyebrows and gesturing with one hand.
Monica brings her hand to her chest.
"Or is that what you're doing with me? Oh, my God. Am I an oat?" Monica asks, her pitch rising on the final question as she stands up.
Richard looks up at her as audience laughter plays.
"You are not an oat," Richard says, shaking his head and standing up.
"I mean, I don't know. I guess I'm just not an oat guy," Richard says, gesturing with his hands.
"I've only slept with women I've been in love with," Richard says. Monica interrupts, saying, "But you've only slept"
"with two people," Monica finishes. Audience laughter plays.
Richard stands still, looking steadily at Monica. Monica stares back.
"Right," Richard says, nodding once.
Monica's eyes widen slightly as she looks up at him.
"Wow," Monica says softly.
Richard maintains eye contact, his expression remaining steady.
"Oh, wow," Monica says, raising her hands near her face. Audience laughter is audible.
She steps closer to Richard, smiling.
She wraps her arms around his neck and says, "You know I love you too, right?"
Richard places his hands on her waist.
"Now I do," Richard says.
They kiss, with Richard's arms wrapped around Monica and her arms remaining around his neck.
The setting changes to a different bedroom. Rachel falls face-forward onto a bed while Ross stands nearby holding a small object. Audience laughter is heard.
"Ross," Rachel says, her voice muffled as she lifts her head.
Rachel quickly stands up. "Ross, please, listen to me. Ross, you are so much better for me than Paolo ever was," Rachel says, walking toward him and gesturing with her hands.
"I mean, you care about me," Rachel continues, as Ross maintains his gaze on the object in his hands.
"You're loving. You make me laugh," Rachel says. "Oh, hey, if I make you laugh, here's an idea," Ross says, raising his hand and pointing.

"Why don't you invite Paolo over to have a little romp in the sack and I'll just stand in the corner until knock, knock," Ross says, making a knocking gesture on "knock, knock" and walking toward a lamp.
Rachel falls face-forward onto the bed again, then quickly sits up, brushing her hair back. Ross turns the lamp off and back on. Audience laughter plays.
"Look, what you and I have is special," Rachel says, stepping toward him with her hands on her chest.
"All Paolo and I ever had was animal sex," Rachel says, extending her arms. "Animal sex?" Ross asks, turning his head toward her.
Ross turns fully to face Rachel, holding his hands out slightly. Audience laughter is audible.
"So what are you saying? I mean, you're saying that there's nothing between us animal at all?" Ross asks, gesturing with both hands.
"I mean, there's not even, like," Ross continues, moving his hands inward slightly.
Ross pauses, keeping his hands raised.
"a little animal?" Ross finishes, leaning forward slightly.
He brings his hands closer together.
"Not even, like, chipmunk sex?" Ross asks, mimicking a small gesture with his hands.
Rachel stands with her hands on her hips, her lips pressed together. Audience laughter plays.
"Okay, Ross, try to hear me, okay?" Rachel says, moving closer to him with her hands raised.
"Hey. I'm not going to lie to you," Rachel says, placing her hands on his shoulders.
"okay? It was good with Paolo," Rachel says, then points a finger near his face. "Knock knock," Ross interjects. "But" Rachel continues.
Rachel lowers her finger and keeps her hands resting on Ross's shoulders. Ross looks at her.
"what you and I have is so much better, okay? We have tenderness, we have intimacy," Rachel says, her hands moving to his face.
"we connect. You know?" Rachel says, holding his face with both hands.
"I swear, this is the best I have ever" Rachel continues, her voice softer as she gazes into his eyes.
Rachel lowers her hands slightly to his shoulders.
"had." Rachel finishes.
Ross maintains eye contact with Rachel.
"Until now," Ross says, turning slightly and stepping back. Audience laughter is audible.
Ross steps up onto the edge of the bed. Rachel places her hands on his waist as they stand close together. Audience laughter continues.
In a brightly lit bathroom, Monica stands near the doorway. Rachel hurries in, shutting the door behind her, and Monica turns toward her.
Rachel leans against the door and says, "Oh, hi."
Monica replies, "Hi," stepping closer to Rachel.
Monica grabs Rachel's arms. "Richard just told me he loved me," she says.
"Oh, my God," Rachel says, and Monica replies, "I know." The two women embrace.
Still hugging, Rachel says, "Honey, that's great." Audience laughter is heard.
The women separate and Rachel bends over the sink, opening a drawer.
"I just can't find other than the top drawer. Hurry," Rachel says, her pace rushed as she rummages.
Monica opens a lower cabinet and searches inside.
Rachel looks at Monica. "You need one too?" she asks. Audience laughter is audible.
Rachel pulls out a small white box. Monica takes the box from her hand and begins to open it. Audience laughter continues.
Monica looks inside the white box, then looks up at Rachel, holding up a single small packet. Audience laughter is audible.
"There's only one," Monica says.
Richard opens the bathroom door, wearing an unbuttoned shirt and boxers. "Monica," he says. Monica quickly hides the packet behind her back and says, "Hi. We'll be right there. We're"
Monica continues speaking, stepping forward and pulling the door shut on Richard. Audience laughter is audible.
The setting changes to the living room. Ross opens a bedroom door and leans out, looking toward Richard who stands in the center of the room.
Ross steps fully into the doorway, leaning against the frame with his hands at his sides, and looks at Richard. Audience laughter continues.
Ross gestures slightly with his hand and speaks. Richard replies briefly. Audience laughter is heard.
Ross points toward the hallway, saying, "They're just trying to decide something," and Richard crosses his arms, replying, "good."
Richard nods, keeping his arms crossed. Ross leans back against the doorframe, remaining silent.
Ross continues leaning against the doorframe, staring at Richard. Audience laughter is audible.
Ross points a finger toward Richard and asks, "So is your mustache used to be different?"
Richard keeps his arms crossed and shakes his head slightly.
"No," Richard replies. Audience laughter is heard.
Ross drops his hand and nods slowly. Audience laughter continues.
Ross gestures with one hand again and asks, "How do you keep it so neat?"
Richard remains still with his arms crossed, maintaining eye contact with Ross.
"I have a little comb," Richard says.
Ross nods again, his expression unchanged. Audience laughter is audible.
Ross asks, "What do you call that?" and Richard replies, "A mustache comb."
"Thank you," Ross says, nodding. Audience laughter is heard.
The setting changes back to the bathroom.
Rachel points at Monica and says, "Okay, I will do your laundry for one month."
Monica shakes her head.
"No," Monica replies. Rachel raises her hands and says, "Okay,"
Rachel gestures widely, pacing slightly, her mouth moving as she stammers.
"I will clean the apartment for two months," Rachel says, touching Monica's shoulders. Monica holds up the packet and says, "All right, I'll tell you what."
Monica holds the packet between them.
"I'll give this to you now if you can tell me where we keep the dust pan," Monica says.
Rachel's mouth drops open slightly. She brings a hand to her face and turns away. Audience laughter erupts.
Rachel leans over the sink, groaning. Monica puts a hand on her hip.
The setting changes back to the living room. Ross continues leaning against the bedroom doorframe, looking at Richard.
Ross asks, "So were you in Nam?"
Richard shifts his stance slightly, keeping his arms crossed, and looks at Ross. Audience laughter is audible. The setting changes back to the bathroom.
Rachel gestures with her hands. "Okay, you know what? You know what? We are both adults here, right?" she says.
Rachel continues, "But there's got to be a mature way to handle this. Rock, paper," raising her fists. Monica agrees, raising her fists.
"scissors," they say in unison, pumping their fists.
They pause with their fists raised.
"One, two, three," they say together, pumping their fists again.
Rachel throws a "rock" gesture while Monica throws "paper." Rachel throws her head back and groans loudly. Monica cheers, throwing her arms up. Audience laughter is audible.
Rachel drops her arms and says, "Fine," looking at Monica.
"Go have sex," Rachel says, gesturing toward the door.
Rachel turns and opens the bathroom door, leaving the room. Monica watches her go. Audience laughter is heard.
The setting changes to the living room. Ross gestures broadly with his hands as he speaks to Richard. "No, you have got it completely wrong. John Savage was Deer hunter," he says.
"No legs. John Voight was coming home couldn't feel his," Ross continues, pointing. Richard interrupts, "No. No way."
Richard steps forward, gesturing with his hands. "You've got it totally the other way around, my friend. John," Richard says. Rachel walks into the living room behind them.
Ross points at Richard, saying, "Voight." Rachel looks toward Ross and calls, "Honey?"
Ross turns toward Rachel.
"What?" Ross asks.
Rachel points a finger toward the bedroom. Ross looks at her, then his shoulders slump slightly. Audience laughter is audible.
Ross turns back toward Richard, pointing a finger at him silently, then turns and slowly walks toward the bedroom. Audience laughter continues.
Richard looks toward the hallway and asks, "Shall we?"
Monica walks out of the hallway. "It's not going to happen," she says.
"They're doing it tonight," Monica adds.
"We can do it tomorrow," Monica says, walking past Richard.
Richard lowers his head, his shoulders dropping. Audience laughter is heard.
Richard looks up and says, "In the future, if I could see the schedule beforehand," gesturing slightly with his hand.
The view shows the exterior of the apartment building at night with city lights visible. Soft instrumental music plays.
The setting changes to a brightly lit coffee shop. Chandler sits on a couch, holding a mug. "so when I woke up this morning, he'd stolen all the insoles out of my shoes," he says.
Chandler takes a sip from his mug.
In an apartment living room, Phoebe, Chandler, and Rachel sit on a couch. Chandler gestures with his hands and says, "Why? Because he thinks I slept with his ex girlfriend and killed his fish."
Phoebe turns her head toward Chandler.
Phoebe asks, "Why would you kill his fish?" Chandler looks at her.
Chandler stares at Phoebe while Rachel looks toward him. Audience laughter plays.
Chandler spreads his hands and says, "Because sometimes, Phoebe, after you sleep with someone you have to kill a fish." Audience laughter continues.
Phoebe places her hand on her chest. Chandler lowers his head.
Rachel places her hand on Chandler's shoulder and says, "Chandler, honey."
Chandler says, "Sorry." Rachel then asks, "Okay?" and Chandler nods.
Rachel gestures forward and says, "Can we watch Joey's show now, please?" Ross and Monica sit on a nearby couch. Ross replies, "Yeah."
Ross says, "He's not here yet." Rachel gestures and responds, "So he's on the show. He knows what happens."
Rachel stands up and walks away from the couch.
Chandler calls out, "Oh, I'm fine about my problem now, by the way." Rachel, standing near the television, says, "Oh, good." Audience laughter is audible.
The television screen displays a scene in a hospital corridor with Joey's character and another doctor walking.
On the television, Joey's character stands in the corridor speaking to a woman. He says, "Amber, I want you to know that I'll always be there for you".
He places his hand on her shoulder and continues, "as a friend and as your".
The woman leans into Joey's character. In the living room, Ross and Monica watch the screen while holding snacks.
Ross looks toward Monica and says, "day."
Ross continues, "Huh? First, the medical award."
On the television screen, Joey's character and the other doctor stand in the corridor.
On the TV screen, Joey's character speaks to the other doctor. He says, "Yeah, some guys are just lucky, I guess." Both men smile.
The woman approaches the two men near the elevator. A PA announcement chime sounds.
An overhead voice announces, "Dr. Ramore, report to first floor emergency staff."
Joey's character lowers his gaze, his smile fading.
Joey's character looks at the other doctor and says, "Well, then".
Joey's character pauses, looking between the other doctor and the woman.
Joey's character steps toward the elevator and says, "guess that's me."
He turns back to face the other two.
Joey's character gestures toward the elevator and asks, "Anyone else need to go on the elevator? Dr."
He points and says, "Wong? Dr."
Joey's character says, "Huang?" The other doctor shakes his head and replies, "No. No, they only said you."
Joey's character stares straight ahead, his facial expression rigid.
He looks down and says, "Okay."
He reaches toward the elevator button.
The woman says, "All right. I love you, Drake." Joey's character turns away and replies, "Yeah, whatever."
The elevator doors open behind Joey's character.
He steps backward toward the open doors. The woman cries, "Oh, no."
He turns around toward the empty elevator shaft.
The other doctor gestures forward and shouts, "Drake, look out."
Joey's character steps into the empty shaft and falls, accompanied by a dramatic musical chord. In the living room, Phoebe, Chandler, and Rachel flinch and gasp.
Ross looks up, his mouth open, and asks, "Did they just kill off Joey?"
Monica replies, "No."
Phoebe, Chandler, and Rachel stare silently at the television. Phoebe holds her hands near her mouth.
Ross asks, "Now?"
Monica responds, "Maybe."
The setting changes to an apartment. Joey lies motionless on a red leather couch, arms crossed over a purple fuzzy pillow. Knocking is heard.
Chandler calls from off-screen, "Come on, open up. We want to talk to you."
Joey, lying still, says, "I don't feel like talking." Rachel adds from off-screen, "Oh, come on, Joey. We care about".
Rachel continues from off-screen, "you. We're worried about you." Chandler adds, "And some of us really have to pee." Joey remains lying down with his eyes closed.
Joey sits up, tosses the purple pillow aside, and walks toward the front door.
Joey opens the door. Rachel rushes inside past him, saying, "Sorry." Chandler enters and says, "Joey."
The rest of the group enters the apartment. Joey stands near the door, looking downward.
Phoebe says, "Listen, sorry about your death. That really sucks."
Chandler adds, "Yeah, we came over as soon as we saw." Ross sits on the red couch and asks, "How could you not".
Ross finishes, "tell us?" Joey, now sitting back on the couch, replies, "I don't know. I was kind of hoping no one would ever find out." He keeps his gaze lowered.
Ross leans forward, resting his hands together.
Ross says, "Well, maybe they could find a way to bring you back."
Joey shakes his head slightly.
Joey says, "They said that when they found my body my brain was so smashed in that the only doctor who could have saved me was me," gesturing toward himself. Audience laughter plays.
Joey stares forward, his facial expression neutral.
Joey gestures slightly and adds, "Supposed to be some kind of irony." Audience laughter continues.
Chandler, standing behind the couch, looks downward while Phoebe looks at Joey.
Phoebe, sitting beside Joey, says, "But, Joey, you're going to be fine. You don't need that show. It was just a dumb soap opera."
Joey turns to her and says, "Phoebe, this was the greatest thing that ever happened to me."
Phoebe quickly replies, "Yes, I was going to incorporate that," nodding. Audience laughter plays.
Joey smirks slightly, then looks away. Monica walks out from a hallway.
Rachel points and says, "Oh, good. Here's Monica. She'll have something nice to say."
Monica stops in front of the group, looking at Joey.
Monica points her thumb behind her and says, "I straightened out your shower curtain so you won't get mildew."
Ross stares at Monica.
Monica spreads her hands and says, "But to me, that's nice." Audience laughter is audible.
Chandler leans forward over the back of the couch.
Chandler says, "It's going to be okay. You know that." Joey looks away and replies, "No, I don't."
Joey raises his hands in front of him.
Joey continues, "It's like you work your whole life for something and you think that when you get it it's never going to be as good as you," gesturing with his hands.
Joey says, "thought it would be. But this so was it changed everything."
He pauses, raises a finger, and adds, "Like the other day, I got this credit card,"
Joey adds, "application and I was," looking forward.
Joey continues speaking as the clip ends.
In a living room, Joey sits on a red sofa wearing a striped robe. Several other people sit around him, including Chandler, Rachel, Ross, Monica, and Phoebe. Joey turns his head toward the group.
"huh? I've never been pre approved for anything in my life." Joey gestures with his hands as he speaks.
Chandler, standing behind the sofa in a dark coat, looks down at Joey.
"I'm sorry, man. Yeah." Chandler says, looking at Joey.
"Joey, honey, I don't know if this will mean anything to you but you'll always be pre approved with us." Monica, sitting next to Joey, speaks and reaches out slightly.
Joey looks at Monica, his facial expression remaining unchanged.
"No, that means nothing to me." Joey replies, shaking his head slightly.
The group sits in silence. Instrumental transitional music plays as the setting changes to a nighttime exterior view of a city skyline with illuminated buildings.
The music ends. Now inside an apartment kitchen, Eddie wears a black turtleneck and holds an oven tray with cookies. Chandler opens the door and walks in.
"Pecan Sandy just made them." Eddie holds the tray out toward Chandler.
Chandler glances at the tray, then looks at Eddie.
"Yeah, all right." Chandler reaches out and picks up a cookie from the tray.
Chandler examines the cookie in his hand, bringing it closer to his face.
"What are these? Raisins?" Chandler asks, looking up at Eddie.
Eddie holds the tray and looks back at Chandler.
"Sure, why not?" Eddie answers, slightly tilting his head.
Chandler tosses the cookie onto a nearby table. Eddie turns away and carries the tray toward the stove.
Chandler steps closer to the kitchen counter while Eddie sets the tray down and turns back to face him.
"Listen, Eddie." Chandler says, looking at Eddie.
Eddie smiles, showing his teeth, at Chandler.
"I've been thinking about our current living situation and why are you smiling?" Chandler asks, gesturing as he speaks, while Eddie continues to smile.
"I got a little surprise. Look, there's," Eddie says, gesturing with his hands and pointing to a fish tank on the counter.
"a new fishy." Eddie says, keeping his hand pointed at the tank.
"I named him Chandler. You know, after you." Eddie points at Chandler as he speaks.
Chandler steps toward the fish tank and leans down to inspect it. Inside the tank, a single goldfish cracker floats in the water.
Chandler stares at the cracker in the tank. Eddie stands nearby, watching Chandler.
"Well, that's not even a real fish." Chandler says, still leaning over the tank before standing up.
Chandler turns to look at Eddie.
"No, that's a goldfish cracker." Chandler states, keeping his eyes on Eddie.
Eddie stares back at Chandler.
"What's your point, man?" Eddie asks, his eyebrows drawing together.
Chandler stares at Eddie for a moment, then turns and walks away from the kitchen area.
"Okay. Good night." Chandler says as he walks toward his bedroom door.
Chandler pauses near the doorway and turns back slightly.
"You big freaking nature." Chandler says before walking out of view.
Upbeat instrumental music begins to play. Text credits begin to appear on screen. The screen turns black.
The music continues. The scene changes to a hallway with a purple door. The door opens, and Ross, wearing a red robe, jogs out.
An uncertain speaker says, "It," as Ross and Richard, wearing a white robe, encounter each other and abruptly stop.
The instrumental music stops. Ross and Richard stand face-to-face, their eyes wide and breathing visibly. Text credits continue to appear on the screen.
They both quickly adjust their robes and shift their gaze, avoiding prolonged eye contact.
Both men stand, shifting their weight. They exchange brief, inaudible greetings as they continue to look away from each other.
Ross rubs his chest and looks downward briefly before looking back at Richard.
Richard says, "brisk tonight." Ross exhales and replies, "Oh, man."
Ross shakes his head slightly.
"Let's never speak of this," Ross says. "You got it," Richard replies. They both turn away from each other.
Ross and Richard walk quickly in opposite directions. Ross opens the purple door and exits the room.
The screen turns black, and a production company logo with text appears.
The screen remains black as the segment ends.
